heading: Definitions
label: 50102 Definitions
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in text, was in the original “this subdivision” probably meaning subtitle B (which probably should have been designated “subdivision” B) of division I of chapter VI of title II of , which enacted this chapter.Pub. L. 98–473
CodificationSection was formerly classified to , The Public Health and Welfare, prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.section 10502 of Title 42

children.text: the term “Federal law enforcement assistance” means funds, equipment, training, intelligence information, and personnel,

children.label: C
children.text: the Criminal Division of the Department of Justice,

children.label: L
children.text: the National Security Division of the Department of Justice,

children.label: M
children.text: the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms, and Explosives, Department of Justice, and

children.label: N
children.text: other Federal agencies with specific statutory authority to investigate violations of Federal criminal laws,

children.label: A
children.text: the perceived need for planning or other activities related to crowd control for general public safety projects, or

children.label: B
children.text: a situation requiring the enforcement of laws associated with scheduled public events, including political conventions and sports events, and

the term “State” means any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, the Virgin Islands, Guam, American Samoa, the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands, or the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.
